alternatives.name: Google Cloud Run Admin API REST reference

gaps: The official gcloud run surface is strong for deploy/update workflows but is not a small standalone API-shaped CLI with generated command coverage for services, jobs, executions, tasks, operations, revisions, and IAM helpers.
gcloud output filtering is powerful, but agents still need to know the right command group and shape; a dedicated which command lowers command-discovery cost for Cloud Run resource work.
The official CLI does not maintain a local SQLite cache for offline search, SQL inspection, or repeated analysis of synced services, jobs, revisions, executions, and tasks.
Small third-party Cloud Run tools found in GitHub search are narrow deploy or monitoring helpers rather than broad Admin API wrappers.
patterns: Google's official CLI uses project and region context, supports machine-readable formatting, and has mature auth through gcloud.
Cloud Run operators frequently inspect services, jobs, revisions, executions, and tasks together while debugging rollout or job execution state.
Read-only list/get/status operations are safe candidates for agent use when token output is compact and selectable.
Long-running operations and job executions benefit from workflow commands that combine resource lookup with operation/execution status checks.

pain_points: users must translate Admin API resource names into gcloud command groups
not an Admin API command mirror

unmet_features: offline search and SQL over Cloud Run inventory
agent-native JSON and field selection by default
command discovery by Cloud Run operator intent
pain_points_to_avoid: requiring agents to parse large default text output
mixing write/delete calls into automatic live checks

novel_features.name: Agent-ready service inventory
novel_features.description: Lists Cloud Run services through the Admin API with agent-friendly JSON, compact output, and field selection.
novel_features.rationale: The official gcloud surface is broad; this focused service inventory command gives agents a direct API-shaped read path with selectable fields.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs a compact inventory of Cloud Run services in a project and region.

novel_features.name: Local Cloud Run inventory
novel_features.description: Syncs Cloud Run resources into a local SQLite store so services, jobs, revisions, executions, tasks, and operations can be searched without repeating every API call.
novel_features.rationale: The official CLI is excellent at live operations, but it does not provide a dedicated local data layer for repeated analysis.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before offline search, SQL inspection, or repeated multi-resource analysis.

novel_features.name: Offline resource search
novel_features.description: Searches synced Cloud Run inventory locally or falls back to live API calls depending on the data-source mode.
novel_features.rationale: Cloud Run debugging often starts with partial names or fragments; local full-text search reduces repeated API calls and context size.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need to locate Cloud Run resources by remembered fragments.

novel_features.name: Cloud Run analytics
novel_features.description: Runs local analysis against synced Cloud Run data for inventory and status-oriented questions.
novel_features.rationale: The Admin API exposes raw resources; operators still need compact summaries to spot stale revisions, failed executions, or inventory shape.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this after sync when the question is about trends or inventory health rather than a single resource.

novel_features.name: Compound Cloud Run workflows
novel_features.description: Provides compound workflows that combine multiple Cloud Run Admin API operations into one operator-facing flow.
novel_features.rationale: Services, jobs, executions, revisions, and operations are related during rollouts and job runs; workflow commands keep those checks together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the task spans more than one Cloud Run resource type.

narrative.headline: A focused Cloud Run Admin API CLI with agent-native output, local inventory, and command discovery.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI wraps the Cloud Run Admin API directly for services, jobs, executions, tasks, operations, revisions, and IAM helpers. It complements gcloud by giving agents a smaller API-shaped surface with JSON-first output, field selection, local sync, search, analytics, and workflow commands.
narrative.auth_narrative: Cloud Run Admin uses Google OAuth bearer tokens with the cloud-platform scope. For local use, run `gcloud auth print-access-token` and export the value as `CLOUD_RUN_ADMIN_OAUTH2C`; do not commit the token or paste it into docs.

quickstart.comment: Confirm whether an OAuth token is already available before calling the Admin API.
Find the exact command path for a Cloud Run task.
List services with selected fields for agent-friendly output.

troubleshoots.symptom: Cloud Run returns authentication or permission errors
troubleshoots.fix: Refresh the token with `gcloud auth print-access-token`, export `CLOUD_RUN_ADMIN_OAUTH2C`, and confirm the account has Cloud Run Viewer or a role with the required Admin API permission.
Use Google resource names such as `projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/REGION` for parent list calls and full resource names for get/update/delete calls.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs focused Cloud Run Admin API access without navigating the full gcloud surface. It is strongest for read-heavy inspection, local inventory sync, search, field-selected JSON, and workflows that correlate services, jobs, executions, revisions, tasks, and operations.

recipes.title: Find the right Cloud Run command
recipes.explanation: Use intent search before calling a specific Cloud Run Admin command.

recipes.title: List services with compact agent output
recipes.explanation: Combines agent defaults with field selection so agents avoid parsing large service payloads.

recipes.title: Search synced inventory
recipes.explanation: Search local Cloud Run inventory after a sync.
